data.title: Trump says Iran breached ceasefire but insists deal will be reached
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran has committed a “serious violation” of the ceasefire, but indicated that a broader peace agreement remains within reach, according to ABC News.

“It will happen. One way or another. The nice way or the hard way. It’s going to happen. You can quote me,” ABC reporter Jonathan Karl quoted Trump as saying Sunday in a post on X.

Trump’s comments followed Iran’s decision over the weekend to again close the Strait of Hormuz, after previously announcing the vital shipping lane had reopened. Tehran said the move was a response to an ongoing U.S. blockade.

data.title: Trump threatens again to blow up all Iran's bridges and power plants
data.description:
President Donald Trump issued a fiery statement Sunday accusing Iran of violating a ceasefire agreement after Iranian forces fired upon ships in the Strait of Hormuz, including a French vessel and a British freighter.

“Iran decided to fire bullets yesterday in the Strait of Hormuz — A Total Violation of our Ceasefire Agreement!” Trump posted, adding that his representatives would travel to Islamabad, Pakistan for negotiations.

The president highlighted what he characterized as Iran’s self-defeating strategy, noting that while Iran recently announced closure of the strategic waterway, the U.S. blockade had already effectively shut it down. Trump claimed this costs Iran $500 million daily while “The United States loses nothing.”

In his most direct threat, Trump warned that if Iran refuses the deal being offered, the United States would “knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran.”

“NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!” Trump declared. “They’ll come down fast, they’ll come down easy and, if they don’t take the DEAL, it will be my Honor to do what has to be done, which should have been done to Iran, by other Presidents, for the last 47 years.”

The president concluded his statement with a call to end what he termed “THE IRAN KILLING MACHINE,” signing off as “President DONALD J. TRUMP.”

The escalation comes as ships reportedly redirect to U.S. ports in Texas, Louisiana, and Alaska to load supplies, a development Trump attributed to actions by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic halts again as U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline nears
data.description:
Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz largely stopped Sunday after Iran reimposed control over the key oil transit route, renewing fears of a prolonged energy shock just days before a fragile ceasefire with the United States is set to expire, Reuters reported.

Iran and the U.S. both said recent talks had shown progress, but neither side gave details. Iranian negotiator Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said major gaps remain over Tehran’s nuclear program and the future of the strait.

Iran had briefly agreed to reopen Hormuz, then reversed course Saturday, accusing Washington of violating the ceasefire by maintaining a blockade on Iranian ports. Vessel tracking data showed almost no ships entering or leaving the Gulf after midnight GMT Sunday. Two ships linked to India reportedly came under attack while attempting passage.

The war, now in its eighth week, has disrupted one of the world’s most important shipping lanes, which normally carries about one-fifth of global oil supplies. Prices have surged as exports remain constrained.

Diplomatic efforts in Islamabad to revive direct U.S.-Iran negotiations continued, though no new round of talks has been scheduled. President Donald Trump said discussions were “very good,” while also warning military strikes could resume if no broader agreement is reached before the ceasefire ends Wednesday.

Hundreds of vessels and roughly 20,000 seafarers remain stranded in the Gulf awaiting safe passage, according to shipping sources cited by Reuters.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: TSLA, IBM, T, INTC, GE, BA, UNH, NOW, PM, CMCSA, and more
data.description:
The upcoming week brings a heavy slate of earnings across sectors, offering a broad snapshot of economic momentum, corporate spending, and global demand trends. From big tech and industrial giants to financials, healthcare, and energy, the reporting calendar is packed with market-moving names.

In technology and AI, results from Tesla (TSLA), Intel (INTC), International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), ServiceNow (NOW), Texas Instruments (TXN), Lam Research Corporation (LRCX), and Vertiv Holdings (VRT) will be closely watched for signals on semiconductor demand, enterprise tech spending, and AI infrastructure buildout.

Industrial and defense names, including General Electric Company (GE), The Boeing Company (BA), Honeywell International (HON), 3M Company (MMM), Union Pacific (UNP), Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX Corporation (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC), will provide insight into global manufacturing, defense spending, and supply chain dynamics.

In telecom and media, AT&T (T), Verizon Communications (VZ), and Comcast (CMCSA) will offer updates on subscriber trends and pricing power. Meanwhile, airlines such as American Airlines Group (AAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), and United Airlines Holdings (UAL) will serve as a read on travel demand and cost pressures amid geopolitical uncertainty.

Consumer and healthcare bellwethers like The Procter & Gamble Company (PG), Philip Morris International (PM), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO), and Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) will shed light on-demand resilience, pricing trends, and healthcare utilization.

Financials and real assets, including American Express Company (AXP), Blackstone (BX), Annaly Capital Management (NLY), AGNC Investment (AGNC), Digital Realty Trust (DLR), and Crown Castle (CCI), will provide perspective on credit conditions, real estate demand, and capital flows.

Energy and materials players such as SLB N.V. (SLB), Halliburton (HAL), Kinder Morgan (KMI), NextEra Energy (NEE), Freeport-McMoRan (FCX), Newmont (NEM), Dow (DOW), and Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) will reflect commodity trends and industrial demand, while emerging tech names like QuantumScape Corporation (QS) add a forward-looking angle.

With such a wide cross-section of the economy reporting, the week’s earnings could provide one of the clearest reads yet on growth, inflation, and market direction heading into the next phase of the year.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of April 20 to April 24:

Monday, April 20

AGNC Investment (AGNC)

AGNC Investment Corp. (AGNC) is set to report Q1 results after Monday’s close, with analysts expecting earnings to decline ~15% despite a sharp ~145% Y/Y increase in revenue.

Both Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts maintain a Buy rating, supported by the stock’s 27%+ gain over the past year.

SA contributor Forward Analytics maintains a Buy on AGNC Investment Corp. (AGNC), with a risk-adjusted price target of $12.51 by 2027 and an estimated total return of ~44%.

The author points to AGNC’s $94.8B portfolio, over 85% allocated to government-backed agency MBS, as a key strength, helping limit default risk amid macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty. The analyst also highlights the rebound in tangible book value per share to $8.9 in Q4 2025, supported by stable net spread income that underpins strong dividend coverage. Even in a downside scenario, the stock is expected to deliver ~15% total return, with a high ~15% dividend yield providing a significant income cushion.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.38
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $390.16M
  • Earnings Insight: AGNC has missed revenue expectations in 8 straight quarters and EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), Alaska Air Group (ALK), Dynex Capital (DX), Steel Dynamics (STLD), Nano-X Imaging (NNOX), and more.

Tuesday, April 21

UnitedHealth Group (UNH)

UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) is set to report Q1 results before Tuesday’s open, with analysts expecting an ~8% Y/Y decline in earnings on largely flat revenue.

The stock, down about 45%, still carries a Buy rating from Wall Street, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains cautious at Hold.

SA contributor Mark Bern, CFA, is constructive, viewing the recent pullback as a compelling long-term entry point. He expects EPS to grow ~12% annually, with potential total returns of ~18% through 2030, supported by a strong balance sheet, scale advantages, and continued market share gains. A 2.9% dividend yield, 16-year growth track record, and ~7.5% expected dividend CAGR further strengthen the investment case.

In contrast, SA contributor Far Horizon maintains a Hold, citing ongoing margin pressure from rising medical costs outpacing reimbursement rates. Headwinds in the Optum segment, including regulatory scrutiny and margin compression, add to the uncertainty. The author expects the stock to remain volatile and range-bound in the near term.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $6.62
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $109.66B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has surpassed EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters and revenue in 5 of those quarters.

Also reporting: GE Aerospace (GE), 3M Company (MMM), Annaly Capital Management (NLY), RTX Corporation (RTX), Halliburton (HAL), Intuitive Surgical (ISRG), United Airlines (UAL), Northrop Grumman (NOC), Danaher (DHR), D.R. Horton (DHI), Capital One Financial (COF), Tractor Supply (TSCO), and more.

Wednesday, April 22

Tesla (TSLA)

Headlining the week, Tesla (TSLA) is set to report Q1 results after Wednesday’s close, with analysts expecting ~32% Y/Y earnings growth on ~16% revenue expansion.

Recent sentiment has been mixed. UBS Group AG upgraded the stock to Neutral from Sell, with analyst Joseph Spak pointing to a more balanced risk-reward as valuation resets reflect near-term EV demand challenges alongside long-term “physical AI” opportunities. However, he expects continued volatility amid concerns around demand softness, rising costs, and heavy capital spending.

Ratings remain sharply divided:

Wall Street leans Buy, SA analysts rate Sell, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system is at Hold, highlighting valuation concerns.

On the bearish side, SA contributors Agar Capital and The Value Portfolio maintain Strong Sell views, citing weakening deliveries, rising inventory, margin pressure, and elevated capex, alongside limited free cash flow growth.

In contrast, SA contributor The Asian Investor upgraded TSLA to Buy, arguing that recent price weakness and negative sentiment have made valuations more attractive. The long-term thesis remains anchored in AI, robotics, and autonomous driving, with upcoming updates on robo-taxi and Optimus seen as potential catalysts.

With fundamentals under pressure but long-term optionality intact, Tesla’s Q1 print is likely to be a key test of whether execution can catch up with expectations.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.36
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $22.34B
  • Earnings Insight: Tesla has exceeded EPS twice in the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates in 50% of those reports.

Also reporting: AT&T (T), Boeing (BA), International Business Machines (IBM), Kinder Morgan (KMI), Philip Morris International (PM), Southwest Airlines (LUV), Texas Instruments (TXN), Quantumscape (QS), Lam Research (LRCX), ServiceNow (NOW), Crown Castle International (CCI), Otis Worldwide (OTIS), Las Vegas Sands (LVS), CSX (CSX), Boston Scientific (BSX), Weatherford International (WFRD), United Rentals (URI), CME Group (CME), Moody's (MCO), and more.

Thursday, April 23

Intel (INTC)

Intel Corporation (INTC) is set to report Q1 results after Thursday’s close, with analysts expecting earnings to plunge ~94% Y/Y on a modest revenue decline.

The company recently introduced its Core Series 3 processors, targeting AI-ready devices for cost-sensitive segments.

Despite an ~85% YTD rally, both Wall Street and Seeking Alpha’s Quant system maintain a Hold rating, citing valuation concerns.

SA Investing Group Leader JR Research upgraded the stock to Hold, acknowledging a strong turnaround but warning that valuation has outpaced execution. While optimism around Intel’s role in AI, particularly in CPUs, foundry services, and advanced packaging, has driven the rally, the stock now trades at elevated multiples, with further upside dependent on tangible progress.

On the bullish side, SA Investing Group Leader James Foord sees Intel as the next Micron, emerging as a key bottleneck in the AI supply chain, supported by heavy investments in fabs and advanced packaging technologies. However, risks remain around execution, competition from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited ((TSM)), and the need to secure external foundry demand.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.01
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $12.38B
  • Earnings Insight: Intel has topped EPS expectations in 4 of the past 8 quarters, beating revenue estimates in 6 of those reports.

Also reporting: Nokia (NOK), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Freeport-McMoRan Copper & Gold (FCX), American Airlines Group (AAL), Dow Chemical (DOW), Newmont Mining (NEM), Blackstone (BX), NextEra Energy (NEE), Comcast (CMCSA), Honeywell International (HON), American Express (AXP), Digital Realty Trust (DLR), Union Pacific (UNP), Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO), Sanofi (SNY), PENN Entertainment (PENN), SAP SE (SAP), Baker Hughes (BKR), and more.

Friday, April 24

Procter & Gamble (PG)

The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) is set to report FQ3 results before Friday's open.

The company recently announced a 3% dividend increase to $1.0885 per share.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains more cautious at Hold, citing slowing growth and valuation concerns.

SA contributor Passage Research, who rates the stock as Buy, expects a potential earnings beat, supported by easier comps in the back half and manageable guidance. While organic sales growth has softened, particularly in Fabric & Family Care, improving comparisons could support modest growth. The stock trades near multi-year low valuations, offering a ~3% dividend yield and ~5% free cash flow yield, though risks from volume pressure, tariffs, and sector rotation remain.

In contrast, SA contributor Justin Purohit maintains a Hold, viewing PG as a defensive play amid market volatility. While Staples' leadership provides stability, recent price weakness and a moderate growth outlook suggest limited upside in the near to medium term.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.56
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $20.54B.
  • Earnings Insight: Intel has topped EPS expectations in 8 consecutive quarters, beating revenue estimates in 3 of those reports.
Also reporting: SLB (SLB), Charter Communications (CHTR), Norfolk Southern (NSC), HCA Healthcare (HCA), Western Union (WU), and more.

data.title: Greg Abel puts his stamp on Berkshire Hathaway as Buffett era fades
data.description:
Greg Abel is beginning to reshape Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) (BRK.A) as he settles into the chief executive role long held by Warren Buffett, signaling a more active management style while promising to preserve the company’s core culture, The Wall Street Journal reported.

At a December employee lunch in Omaha, Abel dismissed speculation that Berkshire would move its longtime headquarters from Nebraska. But inside the company, many recognize that change is underway.

Since taking over in January, Abel has promoted executives from his prior conglomerate operations, restarted Berkshire’s (BRK.B) (BRK.A) stock buyback program, increased the company’s presence in Japan and taken a closer look at both subsidiaries and investments. People familiar with the company say Abel is expected to be more demanding of underperforming businesses and executives than Buffett was, the Journal reported.

Abel, 63, said his leadership style differs from Buffett and the late Charlie Munger, though Berkshire’s (BRK.B) (BRK.A) values remain intact. He has pledged to maintain the insurer-centered model, the mix of diverse operating businesses and the company-run investment portfolio.

Based in Des Moines, Iowa, Abel frequently commutes to Omaha and spends much of his week visiting Berkshire subsidiaries around the country. He has also focused heavily on learning the insurance operations led by Ajit Jain, who remains in charge of that business.

Unlike Buffett, who often tolerated weak managers to avoid confrontation, Abel is viewed as more willing to intervene, replace leaders or even sell businesses that fail to meet expectations. Investors say a major test will be whether he can effectively deploy Berkshire’s (BRK.B) (BRK.A) massive $373 billion cash reserve during the next downturn.

data.title: Utilities plan $1.4T in capex over next five years to upgrade grid, power AI boom
data.description:
U.S. investor-owned utilities have raised their capital spending plans over the next five years by more than 20%, as they seek to upgrade an aging power grid and meet rising electricity demand for the AI boom, according to a new report this week from energy consumer non-profit PowerLines.

The group reviewed 51 recent utility quarterly earnings calls and estimated the companies plan to spend $1.4T through 2030, up from $1.1T in capex plans last year, PowerLines said, warning the growing total “could become a major driver of future rate increase requests."

The utility spending increases "have not yet fully hit people's bills," PowerLines Executive Director Charles Hua said in a discussion of the report, noting that capex and rate increases are "both leading indicators for where the trends around utility and electricity affordability could go over the next coming years."

Electricity cost increases are outpacing many other types of inflation in the U.S., up 4.6% Y/Y in March, compared to a 3.3% gain in overall consumer prices, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

The southeastern U.S. is "a particular hot spot of proposed capital spending," Hua said, with utility capex plans totaling $572B, more than double any other region, the report found.

PowerLines said about half of the proposed spending from the 51 utilities would go toward upgrading the transmission system, much of which is decades old; such work often has proved expensive and lengthy because of permitting issues, regional opposition, and supply chain backlogs.

ETFs: (XLU), (DTCR), (IDGT), (TRFK), (VOLT), (VPU), (FUTY), (IDU), (RSPU), (UTES)

data.title: SA Asks: Is Dell an attractive takeover target?
data.description:
Shares of Dell Technologies (DELL) surged earlier this week on a media report that Nvidia (NVDA) was in talks to acquire a major PC maker, a rumor that Nvidia (NVDA) later denied.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Chris DeMuth Jr., Uttam Dey, JR Research, and Summit Research if Dell (DELL) was an attractive takeover target.

Chris DeMuth Jr.: Michael Dell controls almost 60% of Dell (DELL), so they’ll sell only when he’s good and ready. It can’t be a hostile or even opportunistic bid. He’s savvy and would get a high price. There couldn’t be that many bidders, but the list could include Nvidia (NVDA). Other Nvidia customers might not like it, and antitrust authorities might not like it, either. Such a deal is interesting in theory but probably won’t and probably shouldn’t happen in practice, at least not anytime soon.

Uttam Dey: It's highly unlikely that Dell (DELL) is looking to sell itself. Especially with its shares reaching new all-time highs. Even if Dell is remotely interested in seeking a buyer, the acquisition process would face heightened regulatory scrutiny because it is a large American technology firm.

Dell (DELL) trying to offload its PC business might make more business sense. At CES26 this year, they announced a “PC reset” where they simplified their product lines into three categories to address three broad markets: Dell for consumers, Dell Pro for commercial, and Dell Pro Max for professionals. 

The PC business has been a drag on top-line growth, especially Dell's EBITDA, compared to Dell's infrastructure business. So I see a reason why Dell would try to pull a Micron-like stunt, spinning off their consumer PC business to focus entirely on AI infrastructure.

JR Research: Could anyone else bid for Dell (DELL)? The company would have to be able to reasonably absorb an entity of Dell's size, have consumer-enterprise integration, and be indeed integrating across the stack. And the deal would have to make financial sense. Bidders aren't aplenty at this scale and rationale. If Nvidia (NVDA) is really not interested in dealing, I'm not sure right now who else can and is willing to do so. 

Dell's margins aren't the most attractive, and at $127B market cap, it's nearly 30% of Advanced Micro Devices's (AMD) market cap of $454B. So, even if AMD wants to deal, I don't think it makes financial sense for them, especially since they are still trying to scale up their AI racks (already hitting their margins in the near term).  

Summit Research: Despite rumors of Nvidia interest earlier this week, Dell (DELL) is unlikely to emerge as a takeover target—and neither would that be in the interest of shareholders. The company is currently very well-positioned to capitalize on the next phase of large-scale AI infrastructure deployments. 

In addition to AI-optimized servers, Dell’s storage IPs and traditional server offerings are expected to make a comeback given their increasing relevance in scaling high-volume inference workloads. These higher-margin offerings are expected to bolster Dell’s earnings profile and underscore prospects of a further re-rate from current levels as the secular AI opportunity evolves.

Chris DeMuth Jr. leads the SA investing group Sifting the World, while JR Research runs the investing group Ultimate Growth Investing. 

  • Top Technology Hardware, Storage, and Peripherals Stocks

data.title: Key deals this week: American Express, Otis, Vertiv, Wipro and more
data.description:
Here's a list of key deals reported across sectors this week:

  • American Express (AXP) agreed to acquire Hypercard, known as Hyper, to add to its AI expertise and expense management capabilities, the company said Thursday.
  • Wipro Hydraulics, a part of Wipro (WIT), has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Italy-based Indeco Ind Spa, the companies said on Thursday, marking the Indian firm’s entry into the hydraulic attachments segment.
  • ESCO Technologies (ESE) down 2.7% post-market Wednesday after saying it agreed to acquire Megger Group Ltd. from TBG AG for $2.35B, consisting of $900M in cash and ESCO equity valued at ~$1.4B.
  • Austria-based BAWAG (BWAGF) (BAWAY) is set to acquire the entire issued and to-be-issued share capital of Permanent TSB (ILPMF) (ILPMY) for ~€1.62B, or about $1.91B.
  • Activist investors Bradley L. Radoff and Michael Torok (the Radoff-JEC Group), holding a 7.6% stake in Seer, Inc. (SEER) have launched a formal bid to acquire the company for $2.25 per share in cash. The offer represents a 33% premium to the most recent close and includes a Contingent Value Right.
  • Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) on Monday said it acquired a majority stake in WeMaintain, a technology-enabled elevator and escalator service company, the firms said Monday. Financial terms of the agreement weren’t disclosed.
  • Somnigroup International (SGI) has agreed to acquire Leggett & Platt (LEG) in an all-stock transaction worth ~$2.5B, based on Somnigroup's April 10 closing price.
  • Vertiv (VRT) has acquired BMarko Structures, a U.S.-based provider of custom-engineered structural fabrication. Founded in 2014, BMarko specializes in custom-engineered builds, including steel and wood frames that align well with AI factory and data center requirements.
  • Credo Technology Group (CRDO) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire DustPhotonics, a leading developer of Silicon Photonics Photonic Integrated Circuit (SiPho PIC) technology for optical transceivers, in a cash-and-stock deal.

data.title: American Airlines denies merger talks with United
data.description:
• American Airlines (AAL) announced that it had not engaged in discussions with United Airlines (UAL) regarding a merger and that it was not interested in such a deal, sending the company's stock lower in post-market trading on Friday.
  • Shares of the Texas-based carrier recorded four consecutive sessions of gains this week after Bloomberg reported on Monday that United's (UAL) CEO Scott Kirby, in discussions with senior government officials, floated the idea of a potential combination with the company.
  • "While changes in the broader airline marketplace may be necessary, a combination with United would be negative for competition and for consumers," American (AAL) said.
  • Such a transaction would not be consistent with the company's views regarding the Trump administration's policies on anticompetitive practices, the company added.

data.title: SA Asks: After a post-guidance sell-off, what's next for Netflix?
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares tumbled nearly 10% Friday, the day after the company released its Q1 earnings report along with weaker-than-expected guidance and announced that co-founder Reed Hastings would not be standing for re-election as chairman. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Vinay Utham, Eugenio Catone, Luca Socci, and Pearl Gray Equity and Research what they thought lay ahead for the streaming giant, especially in the wake of its failed takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). 

Vinay Utham: In my opinion, while NFLX Q1 revenues and EPS both beat estimates, to call the EPS a beat would be flattering—strip the $2.8B WBD termination fee out, and the underlying EPS would be closer to $0.70, which would actually be a miss. Critically, what was a bit surprising to me was that NFLX management left the FY26 guidance unchanged despite the March price hike. It also didn't help that the Q2 revenue and EPS guide both came in lighter than estimates. 

The selloff is hardly a surprise given the soft Q2 guide and the unchanged full-year outlook. It is also broadly in line with the thesis that I laid out in my Q1 preview, where I downgraded NFLX from a BUY to a HOLD, arguing that at roughly $99, the name offered no meaningful cushion for disappointment. 

Looking ahead, however, the long-term thesis remains intact, in my view. Ad revenues are on track to hit management's goal of ~$3B, and the H2 margin recovery is already baked into the pricing cadence and content amortization curve. Having said that, I still maintain my view that the risk/reward ratio only becomes favorable in the mid-$80s, subsequently making the range my accumulation zone. I am not planning to sell my NFLX shares on the back of these results, but I wouldn't be adding to them even at these levels. 

Finally, beyond NFLX, I would also keep an eye on Disney (DIS), Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and Amazon (AMZN). More specifically, how DIS executes on its DTC margins will shape how the street looks at NFLX's own margins story. How fast the WBD-Paramount Skydance (PSKY) merger closes would have an impact on NFLX's content bidding environment. As for AMZN, it remains a quiet threat, particularly around live sports, a category that NFLX management has shown an enhanced interest in recent times, especially after the WBD saga.

Eugenio Catone: Following the Q1 2026 release, the market has punished Netflix (NFLX), bringing the stock back to ~$97 per share. The company issued a great quarter, with revenues growing by 16.2% and operating income up by 18%. However, what concerned investors was the guidance: revenues are expected to grow by “just” 13.5% in Q2 2026. 

As a Netflix shareholder, I am not worried at all about the declining growth rate; it is still in double digits, and the company has more than $6B left to complete the buyback program. I believe this drop is just an opportunity to add this streaming giant, which is getting even more diversified. In addition to TV shows and movies, the future of Netflix is expanding into other forms of entertainment, such as podcasts, live sports events, and a new standalone gaming app for kids. It might soon dominate these markets as well.

Luca Socci: Netflix's (NFLX) best decade is probably ahead of us, with three main drivers of monetization clearly laid before our eyes: subscribers, pricing, and advertising. The Q1 2026 report validated this framing since it announced that ad revenue is going to double to $3B by the end of the year. Netflix has also proven to be disciplined by walking away from the WBD (WBD) deal rather than risking overpaying for it. 

In the near term, I think the clear catalyst is Hastings's chair succession. However, I think his decision also shows that he is confident in the company's future and its management team. After all, the three years after he stepped down from his role as CEO have been incredible for the company, with margin expansion from 20% to above 30% and an increasingly convincing dominance in streaming. 

Over a longer time frame, I think that something else is coming for Netflix. It may sound speculative, but I think that in five years or so, we will see it bidding for WBD-Paramount Skydance (PSKY), or at least for the studio division of the newly forming company that has to face important financial constraints right off the bat. Therefore, sticking to my current price target of $120, I think the price drop represents an opportunity rather than a warning.

Pearl Gray Equity and Research: Netflix's (NFLX) initial post-earnings sell-off reflects the stock's general volatility and systematic trading flows. 

I don't think a succession plan and weaker-than-expected guidance warrant a dramatic sell-off, especially not for a stock secured by an underlying company with a 30%+ margin outlook, sustainable double-digit sales growth, a broadening market presence, and opportunities to develop via avenues such as ads, sports coverage, short-form clips, gaming, and emerging market reach. 

Moreover, I'd highlight the fact that we have moved into a market environment that emphasizes the trajectory of thematic pricing metrics related to earnings quality, sales growth, and stock momentum as opposed to isolated fair value models. I do not foresee Netflix losing its momentum unless either the broader market or Netflix itself suffers from a long-lasting material event.

  • Top Movies and Entertainment Stocks
  • Top Broadcasting Stocks
